question: The Cyclic Prefix (CP) is added in the time domain and does not change the fundamental carrier frequency. True or False?
What is the primary function of the 'Cyclic Prefix' (CP) when dealing with Multipath Delay Spread?
options: It tells the receiver which user is transmitting.
It acts as a buffer to ensure that the 'tail' of one symbol doesn't overlap with the 'head' of the next
It increases the frequency of the carrier
It encrypts the start of the packet.
correct_answers: It acts as a buffer to ensure that the 'tail' of one symbol doesn't overlap with the 'head' of the next

question: What is an isotropic antenna?
options: An antenna that radiates power only in a single narrow beam
A theoretical antenna that radiates power equally in all directions
An antenna used exclusively for 5G mmWave
An antenna that only receives signals but does not transmit
correct_answers: A theoretical antenna that radiates power equally in all directions

question: What information is available from a radiation pattern?
options: The frequency and wavelength of the signal
The data rate in bits per second
A graphical representation of the radiation properties of an antenna as a function of space coordinates
The cost of the antenna hardware
correct_answers: A graphical representation of the radiation properties of an antenna as a function of space coordinates

question: What is multipath fading?
options: The loss of signal due to the battery dying
Fading that occurs in any environment where there is multipath propagation, often creating complex transmission effects in mobile environments
The intentional reduction of signal power to save energy
A security attack where multiple paths are blocked by an attacker
correct_answers: Fading that occurs in any environment where there is multipath propagation, often creating complex transmission effects in mobile environments

question: Which of the following are main sources of loss for mmWave transmission?
What are the advantages of beamforming?
image: [Image comparing legacy antenna coverage vs 3D beamforming with massive MIMO]
options: Higher SNR through highly directional transmission
Interference prevention and rejection (prevailing over CCI)
Higher network efficiency allowing denser deployments
Lowering the carrier frequency for better wall penetration
correct_answers: Higher SNR through highly directional transmission
Interference prevention and rejection (prevailing over CCI)
Higher network efficiency allowing denser deployments

question: Match the four elements of beam management for downlink transmission to their definitions.
options: BS transmits beams in a sequence for UE measurement
UE evaluates the quality of received signals
UE selects the optimal beam pair
UE sends measurement info back to the gNB

correct_answers.A: BS transmits beams in a sequence for UE measurement
correct_answers.B: UE evaluates the quality of received signals
correct_answers.C: UE selects the optimal beam pair
correct_answers.D: UE sends measurement info back to the gNB

question: Match the rate type to its correct definition.
options: Rate at which bits are transmitted (bps)
Number of signal units or symbols per second (baud)

correct_answers.A: Rate at which bits are transmitted (bps)
correct_answers.B: Number of signal units or symbols per second (baud)

question: What is the difference between OFDM and OFDMA?
image: [Image comparing OFDM and OFDMA subcarrier allocation over time]
options: OFDM uses one subcarrier, OFDMA uses many
OFDMA divides subcarriers into groups (subchannels) that can be assigned to different receivers/transmitters
OFDM is only for 2G, OFDMA is only for 5G
correct_answers: OFDMA divides subcarriers into groups (subchannels) that can be assigned to different receivers/transmitters

question: In an attack tree representing a Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attack, which action is considered a 'Leaf Node'?
In the STRIDE model, modifying firmware to include a backdoor is which type of threat?
As signal frequency increases (e.g., moving to 5G mmWave), the signal is generally able to pass through walls more easily. True or False?
An attacker deletes logs of their unauthorized access to hide their trail. Which STRIDE category does this fall under?

Explain the difference between Beamforming and MIMO. From a security perspective, why is digital beamforming considered a 'physical layer security' enhancement compared to omnidirectional broadcasting?
options: MIMO uses multiple antennas for throughput; Beamforming directs signals to users. Security: Beamforming concentrates energy in a narrow 'pencil beam,' reducing the attack surface.
MIMO is for security; Beamforming is for speed. Security: MIMO encrypts the signal 360°.
MIMO directs signals; Beamforming increases bandwidth. Security: Beamforming makes signals look like noise.
There is no difference between the two; they both broadcast omnidirectionally.
correct_answers: MIMO uses multiple antennas for throughput; Beamforming directs signals to users. Security: Beamforming concentrates energy in a narrow 'pencil beam,' reducing the attack surface.

question: Explain how the Cyclic Prefix (CP) in an OFDM symbol is used to mitigate the effects of Inter-Symbol Interference (ISI) caused by multipath fading.
options: The CP increases the power of the signal to punch through walls.
The CP acts as a temporal buffer; delayed echoes fall into the guard interval and do not corrupt the next data symbol.
The CP changes the frequency of the carrier to avoid interference.
The CP tells the receiver which user is transmitting to avoid collisions.
correct_answers: The CP acts as a temporal buffer; delayed echoes fall into the guard interval and do not corrupt the next data symbol.

question: Which component of the STRIDE threat model is concerned with an attacker gaining unauthorized access to sensitive V2I (Vehicle-to-Infrastructure) data like location or speed?
An attacker sets up a Wi-Fi Access Point with the exact same SSID as a nearby coffee shop to intercept user traffic. This is known as:
DSSS (Direct-Sequence Spread Spectrum) uses a 'spreading' sequence to increase the bandwidth of the signal, making it look like low-level noise to unauthorized listeners. True or False?
In a TDMA system, if two users transmit in the exact same time slot on the same frequency, what occurs?
In wireless communications, what does 'Noise' (specifically Thermal Noise / Johnson-Nyquist noise) typically refer to?
options: Random electronic fluctuations in the receiver's hardware
The overhead of the MAC layer headers
Intentional jamming from a bad actor
The sound of wind hitting the antenna
correct_answers: Random electronic fluctuations in the receiver's hardware

question: An attacker uses a device that senses when a control packet is being sent and immediately transmits noise to disrupt only that specific packet. What is this attack called?
In the STRIDE model, an attacker who modifies a firmware update to include a backdoor is committing which type of threat?

In 5G, 'Beam Management' is used to track movement. If an attacker performs a 'Beam-Stealing' attack (redirecting the beam away from the legitimate user), which STRIDE category does this primarily fall under?
